_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description:
You can:
- detect and analyse early trends in society, technology, data or cyber security that could be important for your organisation
- advise the organisation on the implications of societal changes, new technologies and uses of data, such as ethical, security or legal implications
- support teams to identify and implement innovation that responds to and scales with a continually changing landscape
- introduce technologies and methods that address shared problems
Examples of leadership using this skill:
- building trust and credibility with stakeholders by demonstrating the relevance of new technologies or methods to address organisational challenges
- helping people at all levels of your organisation understand the potential benefits and risks of changes
- persuading other leaders to support and invest in innovation
reference_document_element_description: Ability to evaluate and adopt new technologies, frameworks, and tools for data and AI work, assessing maturity, community support, integration requirements, and organizational fit before recommending adoption.
comments: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-A-003 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-T-002 (LS, cosine: 0.481), LS-S-011 (LS, cosine: 0.480).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.6/10 (raw 0.561). Shared vocabulary: [data, support, technologies]. DDaT-only terms [address, advise, analyse, benefits, building, challenges] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, adopt, adoption, ai, assessing, community] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.845 (P=0.826, R=0.865). R exceeds P by 0.039 — the WIDAI KSA's 27 words are well-captured by the DDaT element's 125 words, but the DDaT element contains broader scope not fully reflected in the matched WIDAI KSA.

nli_cross_encoder.significance:
NLI: neutral-dominant (99.4%). "You can:
- detect and analyse early trends in society, technology, data or cyber" and "Ability to evaluate and adopt new technologies, frameworks, and tools for data a" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.500 < STS 0.561 (gap -0.061). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [address, advise, analyse] and [ability, adopt, adoption] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.2%. Shared tokens: [data, support, technologies]. DDaT-only: [address, advise, analyse, benefits, building, challenges]. WIDAI-only: [ability, adopt, adoption, ai, assessing, community].
